claims.statement: A repository binding identifies HEAD, tracked differences, and content digests for every visible untracked file.
A failed or truncated Git observation is reported as unavailable and cannot become exact repository Evidence.

One bounded Git observation returns the complete canonical set of tracked repository-relative paths and its exact digest, or reports unavailable on failure, truncation, malformed paths, duplicates, or resource-limit exhaustion.
A declared command returns explicit exit status, stdout, stderr, signal, and truncation state without deciding acceptance.

Under the declared local-direct-node-posix-v1 support profile, one command invocation returns one bounded schema-v2 observation that keeps support, control trigger, confirmed close, actual exit or signal, and each raw byte stream's capture, observed length, digest, strict UTF-8 validity, and truncation facts orthogonal.

Only a supported direct-argv observation with no control trigger, confirmed close, natural exit code zero, and internally consistent raw-byte stream facts can be classified as successful; unsupported profiles, malformed adapter reports, timeout, cancellation, start failure, signal termination, or unconfirmed close remain non-positive.
